question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the object-equality comparison `Long.valueOf(accountId[0]) == shard` (line 67) where autoboxing produces non-cached Long objects for shard values > 127, causing the shard/realm check to always evaluate false and silently rewriting the operator accountId to a different shard/realm that maps to an attacker-controlled account, allowing the monitor to sign staking-related transactions (e.g., CryptoUpdate to change staking node) on behalf of that account and redirect unpaid staking rewards?
